author: phase-4-coordinator
content:
Implemented reduction methods on Array<T,D> and ArrayView (issue #368). New file ferray-core/src/array/reductions.rs adds:

Whole-array reductions:
- sum() / prod() — for any T: Element + Add/Mul, using Element::zero()/one()
- min() / max() — for T: PartialOrd, with NaN propagation per NumPy via self-comparison reduce_step helper
- mean() — for T: Float
- var(ddof) / std(ddof) — for T: Float, returns None if empty or ddof >= n
- any() / all() — for Array<bool, D>, vacuous truth for empty

Axis reductions (return Array<T, IxDyn>):
- sum_axis(Axis) / prod_axis(Axis) — built on existing fold_axis
- min_axis(Axis) / max_axis(Axis) — manual lane iteration via inner.lanes() because PartialOrd has no neutral element (no T::infinity for ints), so fold_axis with an init value would bias the result
- mean_axis(Axis) — computes sum_axis then divides by axis length

ArrayView mirror impls for sum/prod/min/max/mean.

20 unit tests covering: 1D/2D sum/prod/min/max/mean, axis variants, empty edge cases, integer types, Bessel-corrected variance, std, NaN propagation in three positions (start/middle/end), bool any/all (incl. vacuous truth), and ArrayView dispatch.

Result: 447 ferray-core tests passing (up from 427), full workspace tests pass, clippy clean. Closing as completed.
